plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad exposure.
Trim QQQ to 15% to manage broad index allocation.
Initiate a new position in AAPL at 10% as a top-quality name.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. While growth potential is limited, it offers a more stable portfolio.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Maintain QQQI at 20% for income exposure.
Trim QQQ to 15% to manage broad index allocation.
Initiate a new position in AVGO at 10% for quality tech exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index and quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns with reasonable growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Trim SOXL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure.
Sell SPAL to exit an underperforming leveraged ETF.
Reduce QQQI to 10% to shift focus from income to growth.
Increase SMH to 15% to lean into semiconductor exposure.
Initiate new positions in LEU and GUSH at 10% each, based on high-scoring scanner setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this mode can incur substantial losses quickly if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Increase QQQI to 30% to significantly boost income focus.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and prioritize low drawdown.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes income and broad index exposure, cutting leverage and high-beta names to near zero. While aiming for steady monthly distributions, remember these are variable and NAV can still fall in market downturns.
